Petition For Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem To Settle Claim; Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jamay Lee for petitioner and guardian ad litem Oanh Thi Duong (via Zoom).
Jason Louie for co-administrators Huy Nhat Do and Lam Tran, who were also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Petition for Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem to Settle Claim; and 2) First Account and Report.

The Court noted that the request before it sought authorization to approve a proposed settlement agreement although no executed agreement had been submitted. Ms. Lee explained that, under the guardian ad litem's obligations, she could not waive the minor's rights without court approval, and therefore sought authority before executing the settlement agreement. Counsel represented that the settlement would resolve the accounting objections and allow the estate to be closed shortly thereafter. Ms. Lee confirmed that the guardian ad litem would withdraw her objections to the accounting upon approval of the settlement.

The Court also noted that the supplemental declaration filed on October 31 indicated counsel would seek approval of the settlement by ex parte petition. The Court informed counsel that approval of the settlement agreement will require a fully noticed petition.

Following discussion, the Court took both petitions under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Louie appeared by zoom for and with Lam Tran by zoom and Huy Nhat Do by zoom (co-administrators).
Alexander Biddle (guardian ad litem) appeared by zoom for Oanh Thi Duong (minor).

The court stated that it received the response from Mr. Biddle and that Mr. Biddle has been very attentive, but that it is sanctioning him $250.00 for missing court. Mr. Biddle apologized to the court, stated that has spoken with Jamay Lee, that he received a substitution of attorney on September 6th and signed it, and that Mrs. Lee indicated that she was going to his client. Mr. Biddle also stated that he does not know where he stands at this point and that the substitution was not signed by the client. Mr. Louie stated that there has been significant progress since the last hearing, that Jamay Lee will be new counsel for the guardian ad litem, that he has been in constant contact with Mrs. Lee, and that they have already met and conferred. Mr. Louie also stated that Mrs. Lee is on vacation and out of the Country, that he hopes all of the examiner's notes have been cleared and suggested a four-and-a-half-month continuance. The court reminded Mr. Louie of where it stands with respect to exchange of the property and that there is an order to sell. Further discussion was held regarding the inventory and appraisal having three foreign bank accounts listed, the minor residing overseas and the issue of whether there will be a blocked account or a California Uniform Transfers to Minor's Act ("CUTMA") account. The First Account Current and Report of Administrators is continued to November 13, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Louie is to encourage Mrs. Lee to file the substitution of attorney.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Tan. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-11-2023 Continued To Sep-19-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Biddle appeard by zoom for petitioner.
William Keeler appeared by zoom for Huy Do and Lam Tran (respondents/co-administrators).

Mr. Keeler stated that no offer has been made to buy out the minor's one third share of the San Francisco property, but he has spoken with Mr. Biddle on how to coordinate the Viet Nam residence plus the California property, and that Mr. Biddle's client has indicated that she would like to have an arrangement where she can get the residence where she the guardian ad litem and the minor are residing and get cash out of the San Francisco property that will equalize the minor's one third interest. Mr. Keeler also stated that if they can't work it out, he will ask for an order to mediation, and that he will bring a petition for instructions to see if they can enter into an agreement with the Viet Nam lawyers. Mr. Biddle stated that he has some concern about this pro rata scheme as to whether it would be enforceable in Viet Nam, that there is no assurance that the minor's interest would be protected in Viet Nam, that it would essentially treat the Viet Nam estate and the California estate as one, and that he does not believe this can be done or that it is appropriate. Mr. Biddle also stated that something has to be done, that the decedent died four years ago, and that he is asking for the San Francisco property to be sold. Mr. Keeler provided history as to the transactions that took place with regard to the property and the bank accounts. The court set a deadline of September 5, 2023 for the co-administrators to make a good faith offer to buy out the minor's one third share at a current market value and to show that they have the funds/proof of funding to purchase the San Francisco property. The court also reminded the parties that there is the issue of whether there should be any rental income. Mr. Biddle expressed concern about the costs of the co-administrators taking a loan on the property indicating that the minor should not bear those costs. The Petition to Compel Sale of Real Property is continued to September 19, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.
